name: Hot English Mustard
recipeInstructions:
1. Place dry mustard in a small bowl.
2. Gradually add cold water, mixing thoroughly until smooth.
3. Allow the mixture to stand for at least 10 minutes to develop its flavor.
For Variation:
Use the same method, substituting other liquids such as vinegar, wine, stale beer, milk, or even flat champagne for the water. If the mustard is too hot for your palate, add one teaspoon of sugar to tone down its bite. A teaspoon of heavy cream added also turns down the heat.
